summary: Systems architecture review accepts the M4 scoring engine slice. lib/scoring.ts is a pure synchronous shared boundary with no runtime imports from agents or services, accepts Opportunity scoreHints plus SERP serpWeaknessScoreHint-compatible input, applies the documented weighted formula with round-and-clamp behavior, normalizes inputs deterministically, and returns persistence-ready scoreBreakdown and scoreExplanation structures for later scan orchestration.

review_focus.pure_boundary: PASS: lib/scoring.ts has only a type-only import from lib/schemas and no runtime agents/services/provider/API/UI/DB/env/filesystem/network/Prisma/Next/React coupling.
review_focus.shared_contract: PASS: calculateOpportunityScore() accepts nested scoreHints compatible with OpportunityScoreHints plus top-level scoring values, and separately accepts serpWeaknessScore or serpWeaknessScoreHint for compatibility with SerpAnalysisOutput.serpWeaknessScoreHint.
review_focus.formula: PASS: totalScore uses intent 0.18, monetization 0.16, SERP weakness 0.18, toolability 0.18, user fit 0.14, build speed 0.10, minus risk penalty 0.06, followed by Math.round and 0-100 clamping.
review_focus.normalization: PASS: missing, null, empty, nonnumeric, NaN, Infinity, string-like numeric, fractional, negative, and over-100 inputs are handled deterministically and clamped to integer 0-100 values.
review_focus.risk_behavior: PASS: riskLevel minimum penalties for low, medium, high, and excluded preserve the documented formula while ensuring comparable high-risk opportunities rank below comparable safer opportunities; explanation text also flags high/excluded risk for later policy filtering.
review_focus.persistence_shape: PASS: scoreBreakdown matches the existing ScoreBreakdown persistence shape and scoreExplanation includes deterministic JSON-serializable text for every scoring dimension plus totalScore.
review_focus.scope: PASS: no scan orchestrator, API route, DB persistence, UI, provider setup, network call, cron, auth, paid service integration, agent change, schema change, or ranking page implementation was introduced.

decisions: Accepted the lib/scoring.ts boundary as suitable for later scan orchestrator consumption without importing agent or service runtime code.
Accepted the input contract because it bridges Opportunity Analysis scoreHints and SERP Analysis serpWeaknessScoreHint without requiring current agents to change.
Accepted the numeric formula, final rounding, and clamping behavior as aligned with docs/07_data_model_and_scoring.md.
Accepted deterministic defensive defaults as MVP-safe and documented in README.
Accepted the scoreBreakdown and scoreExplanation output as persistence-ready for the later Opportunity total_score, score_breakdown, and score_explanation fields.
Accepted the focused tests as covering the systems concerns from the prior rubric and the active task acceptance criteria.
risks: The first local npm run build attempt compiled and type-checked but exited 1 because Next could not find .next/server/middleware-manifest.json; a no-edit rerun completed successfully, so this appears to be a transient local Next build artifact issue rather than a scoring source issue.

Risk penalty remains bounded by the documented 0.06 weighted subtraction; later scan orchestration and ranking policy should still filter excluded or regulated opportunities rather than relying only on numeric score.
Deterministic default values are MVP heuristics and may need calibration after real scan and feedback data exists.
Next.js build continues to print existing nonfatal @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc invalid Win32 application warnings before falling back successfully.

tests_run.result: PASS: focused scoring suite passed 6 tests covering formula, clamping, high-risk ranking, dimension ranking, invalid hints, determinism, and explanation shape.
PASS: all 38 tests across 8 suites passed, including the scoring suite.
FIRST ATTEMPT: compiled and type-checked, then failed collecting page data with missing .next/server/middleware-manifest.json.
PASS ON RERUN: Next production build completed, generated static pages, finalized optimization, and exited successfully with existing nonfatal local SWC warnings.

requested_action: Proceed with domain_designer review of scoring semantics, risk penalty behavior, and explanation wording for M4-SCORING-ENGINE-001.
